payload.description:
As an optimizer agent, I want optimizer-facing score documentation and procedure guidance to describe prediction outcome segments explicitly instead of using false-positive/false-negative terminology, so that I can reason about error directions without ambiguity when binary labels are inverted.

Feature: Optimizer score documentation uses explicit predicted/actual segment language

Scenario: Investigating prediction outcome segments in optimizer-facing docs
Given the optimizer-facing score documentation and procedure guidance
When an agent reads about a misclassification direction
Then the docs describe it using explicit predicted and actual labels
And the docs do not rely on false-positive or false-negative terminology

Scenario: Learning a reusable NA-gating pattern from the score docs
Given the umbrella score YAML documentation
When an agent reads about scores with Yes/No outcomes plus an NA case
Then the docs explain applicability gating as a separate first-class pattern
And the subclass docs show concrete implementation recipes for that pattern
payload.title: Optimizer docs use explicit confusion-matrix segment language
